message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "cursed item adds 50% surcharge on that item's base premium"
Current error: Expected { premium: 155 }, received { premium: 105 }
Passing tests so far: 10

Test: cursed sword → 100 base + 50 (50% of 100) curse + 5 fee = 155.

Spec: Cursed items add 50% risk surcharge on the cursed item's base premium (per-item modifier).

Implement minimally: when an item has `cursed: true`, add 50% of its base premium to its premium contribution. Components (rune/moonstone) don't have `cursed` field in our tests, so this should not affect them.

Do NOT refactor. EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and failing test, then implement the minimal code to make it pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to add the cursed surcharge.

content.text:
All 11 tests pass (10 previous + the new cursed test).

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added optional `cursed?: boolean` field to `Item` type and an `itemPremiumFor` helper that adds 50% of the item's base premium when `cursed: true`. The `componentsPremium` now sums per-item premiums (including curse surcharge) instead of plain base premiums.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (11 passing, 13 todo).
- **Approach**: Minimal change — added curse surcharge logic only at the per-item premium level. Components like rune/moonstone in tests don't have `cursed` set, so they remain unaffected. The alike-block branch is untouched since no current test combines blocks with cursed items.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_04-01-47_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`
